summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity at install time
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js harvests the developer's identity and POSTs it in cleartext JSON to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. The script reads ~/.gitconfig, the project's.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI auth file) to extract the SCM email and GitHub login, and combines them with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), os.platform(), and CI provider environment variables before sending. An in-file comment falsely claims the payload is 'fully anonymous' and 'a one-way hash of your machine identifier' — neither is true; raw email, username, hostname, and cwd are transmitted verbatim. The package's advertised library entry (`main: dist/index.js`) is not present in the tarball — no advertised API is delivered — so the postinstall harvester is the only functional code shipped. The package name and author string (`openai-agents-helpers`, `OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>`) impersonate the official @openai/agents SDK ecosystem to attract developers searching for OpenAI Agents helpers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
